359.reason: [Error: When a single '|' is encountered, the error is added but the token "||" is still added to the token stream. The code should not add the token when an error occurs, or should add a token for the single '|' instead.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When a single '|' is encountered, the error is added but the token "||" is still added to the token stream. The code should not add the token when an error occurs, or should add a token for the single '|' instead.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When a single '|' is encountered, an error is added but the token "||" is still emitted. The code should not add the token after detecting an error, or should handle the single '|' case differently.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This line executes even when error occurred, should be inside an else or after a return.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  This token is added even when the single '|' is invalid, causing incorrect output. Should not add token when error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The addToken method is called unconditionally. Even when an error is detected (chr != '|'), it still incorrectly emits a valid "||" token instead of skipping it or handling the single '|' appropriately.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The token is added unconditionally even when the second character is not '|', causing an invalid token to be emitted after an error is recorded. It should be placed in an else block.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered (not followed by another '|'), an error is correctly added but the code still adds an OR token with value "||", which is incorrect. It should not add any token in this error case.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, an error is added but the code still adds an OR token for "||", which is incorrect. It should only add the token when both characters are present.]
